name: Gastroenteritis
notes: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, often related to diet changes, infections, or toxins.
recommendedActions: withhold food briefly as directed by a veterinarian
contact veterinarian if vomiting or diarrhea persists beyond 24 hours
seek emergency care if vomiting is severe or contains blood

name: Pancreatitis
notes: Inflammation of the pancreas that can be acute or chronic and is often underdiagnosed in cats.
recommendedActions: supportive care for pain, nausea, and hydration
monitor for worsening vomiting or lethargy
follow vet instructions on diet modification

name: Hepatic Lipidosis
notes: Fat accumulation in the liver, often triggered by a period of anorexia; common life-threatening liver disease in cats.
recommendedActions: aggressive nutritional support may be required
monitor liver values as directed by veterinarian

name: Inflammatory Bowel Disease
notes: Chronic inflammation of the intestines that often causes waxing and waning GI signs.
recommendedActions: discuss diet trials and long-term management
monitor body weight and appetite trends
